title: Weather Today [20 May, 2026]: Check IMD Rain Predictions, Heat Alert, Snow & Thunderstorms Across Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Jammu, Hyderabad & More — Full City-Wise Forecast - The Sunday Guardian
full_text:
Weather Today, 20 May 2026: India is witnessing two sharply contrasting weather systems at the same time. Northern and central states continue to battle severe heatwave conditions, while southern states are seeing widespread rainfall linked to the advancing southwest monsoon. Temperatures in several cities have crossed 45°C, with Banda in Uttar Pradesh touching a dangerous 47.6°C while, Kerala and coastal Karnataka are receiving consistent rain activity, signalling that monsoon conditions are strengthening earlier than usual.
Will it Rain or Snow in the Hills?
Higher reaches of Jammu and Kashmir may witness light snowfall in isolated regions alongside rainfall activity while srinagar is expected to remain cool compared to the plains temperature as Minimum: 11°C & Maximum: 20°C Tourist areas in Himachal Pradesh and Kashmir could experience cloudy skies and brief showers through the week.
Why Is The Heat Building Up So Fast Across India?
The rapid rise in temperatures is being driven by a persistent heat dome across north and central India. Dry winds from Rajasthan, lack of rainfall, and clear skies are intensifying daytime heating while night temperatures are also staying 5°C above normal in many regions, reducing overnight cooling and worsening heat stress.
Where are the Heat Wave Alerts?
- Delhi, Haryana, Punjab under severe heatwave warning
- Uttar Pradesh temperatures touching 46–47°C
- Rajasthan expected to cross 44°C in multiple districts
- Vidarbha and Madhya Pradesh under heat stress alerts
- Chandigarh likely to touch 47°C next week
- Night temperatures remain unusually high across north India

How Will Rising Temperatures Impact?
Extreme temperatures may increase dehydration, heatstroke cases, electricity demand and pressure on water supplies. Agriculture and outdoor labour sectors could face disruptions while rising nighttime temperatures are also increasing health risks for elderly people and children.

FAQ’s
1. Are there any travel or safety warnings?
Yes. Authorities are advising people to avoid afternoon travel in heatwave-hit states and stay hydrated.
2. Will temperatures rise across the country?
North and central India may continue seeing temperatures above 45°C through May 25.
3. Will there be any rain today?
Southern states including Kerala and coastal Karnataka are likely to receive rainfall.
4. Where will it snow today?
Light snowfall is possible in isolated higher reaches of Jammu and Kashmir.
5. When will monsoon arrive in Kerala?
Current forecasts suggest monsoon onset around May 26, earlier than the normal June 1 timeline.
Disclaimer: Weather forecasts are for guidance only. Conditions may change rapidly. Always check local authorities for emergencies and travel alerts.
